senators.name: Tammy Baldwin
senators.nerdReason: B.A. (double major in government and mathematics) from Smith College and J.D. from University of Wisconsin Law School. Mathematics degree provides quantitative analytical foundation rare among legislators. Career as attorney. First openly gay U.S. Senator. Deep policy expertise on healthcare, trade, and manufacturing built over decades in Congress.
education: J.D., University of Wisconsin Law School
B.A. Government and Mathematics, Smith College

senators.name: Ron Johnson
senators.notNerdReason: B.A. in business and accounting from University of Minnesota and M.B.A. from University of Minnesota. Career as CEO of PACUR (polyester/plastics manufacturing). Business and accounting education provides quantitative skills. However, legislative approach frequently contradicts established scientific consensus on healthcare and climate. MBA credentials not matched by evidence-based governance.
education: B.A. Business and Accounting, University of Minnesota

WI-01.name: Bryan Steil
WI-01.notNerdReason: B.A. from Georgetown University and J.D. from University of Wisconsin Law School. Career as attorney, manufacturing company executive (Charter NEX Films), and aide to Paul Ryan. Georgetown + Wisconsin Law shows intellectual engagement but legislative approach is more politically conventional than evidence-based.
education: J.D., University of Wisconsin Law School

WI-02.name: Mark Pocan
WI-02.notNerdReason: B.A. in journalism from University of Wisconsin-Madison. Career as small business owner (printing company) and state legislator. No analytical or evidence-based policy approach beyond standard progressive advocacy.

WI-03.name: Derrick Van Orden
WI-03.notNerdReason: Attended Winona State University (did not complete degree). Career as Navy SEAL with 26 years of service. Author. Military special operations background but no academic credentials or evidence-based policy approach.

WI-04.name: Gwen Moore
WI-04.notNerdReason: B.A. from Marquette University. Career in social work, community organizing, and state legislature. Policy driven by personal experience and community advocacy rather than analytical or research-based frameworks.

WI-05.name: Scott Fitzgerald
WI-05.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Wisconsin-Oshkosh. Career as newspaper publisher, Army officer, and state legislator (Wisconsin Senate Majority Leader). No analytical or evidence-based policy background.

WI-06.name: Glenn Grothman
WI-06.notNerdReason: B.A. and J.D. from University of Wisconsin-Madison. Career as attorney and state legislator. Wisconsin Law School provides analytical training but governance approach driven by ideology rather than evidence.

WI-07.name: Tom Tiffany
WI-07.notNerdReason: B.S. from University of Wisconsin-River Falls. Career in railroad and oil industry, outdoor recreation. State legislator. No analytical or evidence-based policy background.
experience: Dam Tender, Wisconsin Valley Improvement Company

WI-08.name: Tony Wied
WI-08.notNerdReason: New member of Congress (2025). Career as business owner. Limited public record of analytical or evidence-based policy approach.
